title: WBJEE 2026 Admit Card

detail.title: WBJEE 2026 Admit Card
detail.short_info: WBJEE 2026 admit card has been released on 15 May 2026 at wbjeeb.nic.in. Candidates can download their West Bengal JEE hall ticket using their application number and password. Check the exam date, scheduled for 24 May 2026, along with exam timings, centre address, and important instructions here.

content.text: The WBJEE 2026 exam is scheduled for 24 May 2026 in offline OMR‑based mode for admission to engineering, technology, pharmacy and architecture courses in West Bengal colleges. Candidates who successfully registered can log in at wbjeeb.nic.in and download their hall ticket once the link is activated.

rows: West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board (WBJEEB)
B.E/B.Tech, B.Pharm and other professional UG programmes in West Bengal colleges
Application Number and Password (or Date of Birth, as allowed) plus Security Pin/Captcha

sections.heading: How to Download WBJEE 2026 Admit Card

content.text: Once the link goes live today, candidates can download their WBJEE hall ticket by following these steps:

items: Visit the official WBJEEB website: https://wbjeeb.nic.in and click on the WBJEE 2026 tab.
Click on the “WBJEE 2026 Admit Card” / “Download Admit Card” link on the homepage or candidate login page.
Enter your Application Number and Password (or Date of Birth, if that option is given), then enter the security pin/captcha.
Click on “Sign In / Submit” to access your candidate dashboard.
Click on “Download Admit Card / View Hall Ticket”; your WBJEE 2026 admit card will appear on the screen with exam date, time and centre details.
Check all details carefully and download and print 2-3 clear A4‑size copies of the hall ticket.

content.text: If you forget your application number or password, use the “Forgot Application Number/Password” links available on the login page.

sections.heading: Details Mentioned on WBJEE 2026 Admit Card

content.text: After downloading, candidates should verify the following details:

items: Candidate’s name, photograph and signature.
Application number and WBJEE roll number.
Exam name: WBJEE 2026.
Exam date: 24 May 2026 and paper details (Paper I - Mathematics, Paper II - Physics & Chemistry).
Exam time and reporting time; gate‑closing time (mentioned in admit card).
Exam centre name, code and full address.
Important exam‑day instructions and items allowed/prohibited.

content.text: If any discrepancy is found in the admit card (name, photo, category, exam centre etc.), candidates should immediately contact WBJEEB helpdesk using the contact information on the official website.

sections.heading: Documents Required with WBJEE 2026 Admit Card

content.text: On the WBJEE 2026 exam day, candidates must carry:

items: A printed copy of the WBJEE 2026 admit card (clear A4‑size printout; colour or good‑quality black and white).
One original valid photo ID proof, such as: Aadhaar Card PAN Card Passport Voter ID Driving Licence Any other government‑issued ID mentioned in the information bulletin.
Aadhaar Card
PAN Card
Passport
Voter ID
Driving Licence
Any other government‑issued ID mentioned in the information bulletin.
Additional documents, if applicable (PwD certificate and scribe permission letter for candidates availing scribe facility).

content.text: Entry into the exam centre will not be allowed without the printed hall ticket and valid photo ID.

sections.heading: Exam‑Day Instructions for WBJEE 2026 Admit Card

content.text: To ensure smooth conduct of the WBJEE 2026 exam, candidates should follow these guidelines:

items: Reach the exam centre well before the reporting time mentioned on the WBJEE 2026 admit card so that verification, biometric/ID checks and seating can be completed; late entry after the gate‑closing time may not be allowed.

Carry only essential items: your WBJEE 2026 Admit Card (clear A4‑size printout), a valid photo ID (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, Voter ID, Driving Licence, etc., as permitted) and allowed stationery such as blue/black ball‑point pens and other items specified in the exam instructions.

Do not carry mobile phones, smart watches, calculators (if not permitted), Bluetooth devices, earphones, notes, books or any other electronic gadgets or written material inside the examination hall; such items are strictly prohibited and may lead to cancellation of your paper and other disciplinary action.

Follow all instructions printed on the WBJEE admit card and those announced by the invigilators during the exam; indulging in any unfair means, talking in the exam hall or disobeying exam rules can result in cancellation of your candidature.
Keep your WBJEE 2026 admit card safely even after the exam, as it may be required during counselling, document verification and admission procedures.
